memoryArtifacts.headline: The Garretts -- Before America
memoryArtifacts.description:
The Garretts — Before America
by John E. Garrett
Last year, I published my work entitled, "1000 Years of Garrett Family History". The emphasis in that effort
was the genealogy of the Garretts since their immigration to America in the mid-seventeenth century. This
work examines how the Garretts evolved during the generations that they spent in England, Wales and
Ireland from the time the first ancestor landed in England until the first Garrett came to America.
My research involved the use of:
 A commercial genealogy website named Ancestry.com.
 Extensive use of the Google search engine on the Internet
The first 100 years, starting in the eleventh century, was the most interesting. That was where I found a
remarkable relationship between my ancestors and King Edward the Confessor, King William the Conqueror
and King Henry I. My first ancestor to land in England, Otho Geraldini, grew-up with Edward before he
became King; his son Walter FitzOtho was in William, the Bastard's army; and his son Gerald grew-up with
Prince Henry (King William"s son) who became King Henry I. Their close relationship is demonstrated by the
vast amount of land, castles and titles bestowed upon them.
In retrospect, I am impressed at how important the genes of the spouses are to the family's genealogy. The
best example of this is when my ancestor, Gerald of Windsor married Nesta Rhys, a Welsh Princess. There
offsprings were all outstanding. The next "high" came when the Garrett's became merchants in the 16th
century, opened trade with Russia, became Lord Mayor of London and honorary knighthood.
To make this work more readable, I have placed several interesting "asides" in the Appendix. For example,
the de Clares, of Richard "Strongbow" de Clare fame married into the Garretts. I found extensive genealogy
on the ancestry of Richard "Strongbow" de Clare going back before 100 AD. So, I put his data into two
appendices. One for the French ancestors and another for the Viking ancestors.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle於大約1075年出生在威爾士布洛可爾卡維時，他的父親Walter de Windsor40歲，母親Beatrice de Offley37歲。他於1104年與Nesta ferch Rhys在英國威爾斯布洛可爾布洛可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1095年登記服兵役。他於1102年在英國威爾斯布洛可爾布洛可被列為constable of pembroke castle。他去世於大約1136年在他的家鄉享年63歲，埋葬在威爾士布洛可爾卡維。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle于大约1075年出生在威尔士布洛可爾卡維时，他的父亲Walter de Windsor40岁，母亲Beatrice de Offley37岁。他于1104年与Nesta ferch Rhys在英国威尔斯布洛可爾布洛可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1095年登记服兵役。他于1102年在英国威尔斯布洛可爾布洛可被列为constable of pembroke castle。他去世于大约1136年在他的家乡享年63岁，埋葬在威尔士布洛可爾卡維。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin), German, Dutch, Polish, Swedish, and Danish: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This personal name was introduced into England both in the reign of Edward the Confessor and by the Normans (in the forms Walt(i)er, Waut(i)er). After the Norman Conquest it soon became one of the most popular personal names in Britain. Compare Walther .
Germanized or Americanized form of Czech, Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian Valter and Czech Valtr: from the personal name Valter, Valtr, of ancient Germanic origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Walthers
meanings: German: patronymic from the personal name Walther (see Walter ).

variants.name: McQuate
meanings: Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uait ‘son of Uat’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Irish form McQuaid .

variants.name: Waltke
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Jurgen, Wilhelmina.
German: from a pet form of any of the ancient Germanic personal names formed with Old High German waltan ‘to rule’ as the first element, as in Walter .

variants.name: McWaters
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaitéir, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of the personal name Walter , with excrescent English -s.

variants.name: Wahler
meanings: German:
from the personal name Wol(d)er, Low German form of Walter .
habitational name for someone from any of various places called Wahl, Wahle, or Wahlen.

variants.name: McWalter
meanings: Irish and Scottish: from Gaelic Mac Uaitéir ‘son of Walter ’.

variants.name: Walters
meanings: English: variant of Walter , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
German: patronymic from   Walter  .

variants.name: McQuaid
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Colum, Nuala.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uaid ‘son of Uad’, Gaelic form of the personal name Wat, a short form of Walter . Compare the Scottish form McQuate .

variants.name: Walther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Hans, Erwin, Wolfgang, Otto, Gerhard, Gunter, Heinz, Klaus, Konrad, Bernd, Bernhard.
German, Dutch, Swedish, and Danish: variant of   Walter  .

variants.name: Lauter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Erwin, Kurt, Sigismund, Wolf.

German: topographic name and habitational name, from a common stream name meaning ‘clear’, from Middle High German hlutra, or from any of the places called Lauter or Lauta (Saxony). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and some other European countries.
Germanized or Americanized form of Slovenian Lavtar or its Croatized and rare Slovenian variant Lautar: from a dialect variant of the personal name Valter (see Walter ).

nameMeanings.name: Gerald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gār, gēr ‘spear’ + wald ‘rule’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. There has been some confusion with Gerard . It died out in England at the end of the 13th century. However, it continued to be popular in Ireland, where it had been brought in the 12th century at the time of Strongbow's invasion. It was used in England in the 17th century and revived in the 19th century, along with several other long-extinct names of Norman, Old English, and Celtic origin, and is now more common than Gerard, which survived all along as an English ‘gentry’ name.

variants.name: Jerrold
meanings: Rare variant of   Gerald  , probably a transferred use of the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

en.lifeSummary: When Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle was born about 1075, in Carew, Pembrokeshire, Wales, his father, Walter de Windsor, was 40 and his mother, Beatrice de Offley, was 37. He married Nesta ferch Rhys in 1104, in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He registered for military service in 1095. In 1102, at the age of 28, his occupation is listed as constable of pembroke castle in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom. He died about 1136, in his hometown, at the age of 63, and was buried in Carew, Pembrokeshire, Wales.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle est né environ 1075, à Carew, Pembrokeshire, Galles, son père, Walter de Windsor, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Beatrice de Offley, avait 37 ans. Il a épousé Nesta ferch Rhys en 1104, à Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1095. En 1102, à l’âge de 28 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est constable of pembroke castle à Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé environ 1136, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 63 ans, et a été enterré à Carew, Pembrokeshire, Galles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle um 1075 in Carew, Pembrokeshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter de Windsor 40 und seine Mutter Beatrice de Offley 37. Er hat 1104 in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom Nesta ferch Rhys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er hat sich 1095 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1102, als er 28 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als constable of pembroke castle in Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb um 1136 im Alter von 63 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Carew, Pembrokeshire, Wales begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle è nato il circa 1075 nel Carew, Pembrokeshire, Galles, il suo padre, Walter de Windsor, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Beatrice de Offley, aveva 37 anni. Lui sposato Nesta ferch Rhys nel 1104, nel Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1095. Nel 1102, all'età di 28 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come constable of pembroke castle nel Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Galles, Regno Unito. Morì circa 1136, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 63 anni, e fu sepolto a Carew, Pembrokeshire, Galles.

ja.lifeSummary: Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castleが約1075年にウェールズペンブロケシュアイアカーエウで生まれた時，父Walter de Windsorは40歳，母Beatrice de Offleyは37歳であった。1104年にイギリスウェールズペンブロケシュアイアペンブロクでNesta ferch Rhysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1095年に兵役登録をした。1102年，28歳の時，イギリスウェールズペンブロケシュアイアペンブロクでの職業はconstable of pembroke castleと記録されている。彼は約1136年に故郷で63歳で死去し，ウェールズペンブロケシュアイアカーエウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle(이)가 대략 1075년 웨일스 p브로케실르 카레에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Windsor(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Beatrice de Offley(은)는 37세였다. 그는 1104년 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르 p브로크에서 Nesta ferch Rhys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1095년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1102년, 28세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르 p브로크에서 constable of pembroke castle이었다. 그는 대략 1136년 그의 고향에서 63세의 나이로 사망했으며 웨일스 p브로케실르 카레에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle nasceu aproximadamente 1075, em Carew, Pembrokeshire, Gales, seu pai, Walter de Windsor, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Beatrice de Offley, tinha 37 anos. Ele casou-se com Nesta ferch Rhys em 1104, em Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1095. Em 1102, com 28 anos, sua profissão aparece como constable of pembroke castle em Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1136, em sua cidade natal, com 63 anos, e foi sepultado em Carew, Pembrokeshire, Gales.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle родился около 1075 г. в местности карю, пемброкешир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Walter de Windsor было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Beatrice de Offley, 37. Он женился на женщине по имени Nesta ferch Rhys в 1104 г. в местности пемброк, пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. В 1095 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1102 году, в возрасте 28 лет, работал по профессии constable of pembroke castle в местности пемброк, пемброкешир, Уэльс, Великобритания. Он умер около 1136 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 63 лет и был похоронен в местности карю, пемброкешир, Уэльс.

sm.about 1075–about 1136: e tusa o le 1075–e tusa o le 1136

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gerald Fitz Walter Lord Constable of Windsor Castle nació aproximadamente 1075, en Carew, Pembrokeshire, Gales, su papá, Walter de Windsor, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Beatrice de Offley, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Nesta ferch Rhys en 1104, en Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1095. En 1102, a la edad de 28 años, su ocupación registrada era constable of pembroke castle en Pembroke, Pembrokeshire, Gales, Reino Unido. Murió aproximadamente 1136, en su pueblo, a la edad de 63 años, y fue sepultado en Carew, Pembrokeshire, Gales.
